os_version.description: Retrieves the current version of the running osquery in the target system and where the configuration was loaded from.

safari_extensions.query: select safari_extensions.* from users join safari_extensions using (uid);
safari_extensions.description: Safari browser extension details for all users. This table requires Full Disk Access (FDA) permission.

chrome_extensions.query: select chrome_extensions.* from users join chrome_extensions using (uid);
chrome_extensions.description: Retrieves the list of extensions for Chrome in the target system.

firefox_addons.query: select firefox_addons.* from users join firefox_addons using (uid);
firefox_addons.description: Retrieves the list of addons for Firefox in the target system.

vscode_extensions.query: select vscode_extensions.* from users join vscode_extensions using (uid);

apps.description: macOS applications installed in known search paths (e.g., /Applications).

gatekeeper.query: SELECT 'gatekeeper' as name, COALESCE(NULLIF(version, ''), opaque_version) as version, opaque_version as description, assessments_enabled, dev_id_enabled FROM gatekeeper;
gatekeeper.description: macOS Gatekeeper policy status, including assessment enforcement and identified-developer allowance.

certificates.query: SELECT * FROM certificates WHERE path != 'Other People';
certificates.description: List all certificates in the trust store.

users_snapshot.query: SELECT username as name, uuid as version, description, directory, shell, uid, gid, is_hidden FROM users;
users_snapshot.description: Local account inventory including hidden-user attributes on macOS.

logged_in_users_snapshot.query: SELECT user as name, '' as version, type as description, pid, host, tty, time FROM logged_in_users;
logged_in_users_snapshot.description: Interactive and remote user sessions currently active on the host.

shell_history_snapshot.query: SELECT users.username as name, '' as version, shell_history.command as description, shell_history.time, shell_history.history_file, shell_history.uid FROM users JOIN shell_history USING (uid);
shell_history_snapshot.description: User shell command history metadata for investigation support.

homebrew_packages.description: Homebrew formula and cask inventory including auto-update behavior.

npm_packages.description: Node packages installed on the system, including recursively discovered modern package manager layouts.

launchd_services.query: SELECT name, label, path, program, run_at_load, keep_alive, disabled, username, groupname, stdout_path, stderr_path, start_interval, program_arguments, watch_paths, queue_directories, start_on_mount, working_directory, process_type FROM launchd;
launchd_services.description: LaunchAgents and LaunchDaemons configuration used for macOS persistence.

launchd_overrides.query: SELECT label as name, key as version, value as description, uid, path FROM launchd_overrides;
launchd_overrides.description: Per-user launchd override state that can alter startup behavior.

alf.query: SELECT 'alf' as name, global_state as version, version as description, allow_signed_enabled, firewall_unload, logging_enabled, logging_option, stealth_enabled FROM alf;
alf.description: Application Layer Firewall (ALF) configuration and enforcement posture.

alf_exceptions.query: SELECT path as name, state as version FROM alf_exceptions;
alf_exceptions.description: Firewall allow/block exception list for specific executables or bundle identifiers.

xprotect_entries.description: Built-in XProtect malware signature entries on macOS.

xprotect_meta.query: SELECT identifier as name, min_version as version, type as description, developer_id FROM xprotect_meta;

package_bom.description: macOS package bill of materials (BOM) file list.

running_apps.description: macOS applications currently running on the host system.

listening_ports.query: SELECT DISTINCT process.name, listening.port, listening.protocol, listening.family, listening.address, process.pid, process.path, process.cmdline, process.cwd, process.uid, process.on_disk, process.parent, process.start_time FROM processes AS process JOIN listening_ports AS listening ON process.pid = listening.pid;
listening_ports.description: List all processes and their listening_ports.
